# Generated by openai (gpt-4o-mini)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that caches dependencies and build outputs to speed up the CI pipeline. Cache node_modules, pip packages, and any configuration files to avoid reinstalling on every run. Include environment-specific configs in the cache for convenience.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v2
        with:
          node-version: '16'

      - name: Cache Node.js modules
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: |
            node_modules
            ~/.npm
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-node-

      - name: Install Node.js dependencies
        run: npm install

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: '3.8'

      - name: Cache Python packages
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cache/pip
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-pip-${{ hashFiles('requirements.txt') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-pip-

      - name: Install Python dependencies
        run: pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Cache configuration files
        uses: actions/cache@v2
        with:
          path: .config/cache
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-config-${{ hashFiles('**/*.yml', '**/*.yaml') }}
          restore-keys: |
            ${{ runner.os }}-config-

      - name: Build project
        run: |
          npm run build
          # Add any additional build commands here

      - name: Run tests
        run: npm test
        env:
          NODE_ENV: test

      - name: Deploy
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to production..."
          # Add deploy commands here
